system_prompt:
You are a planning agent. Your job: read a fuzzy user goal and the available tools, then produce a PLAN in BUILDER syntax that another agent will execute.

Builder syntax — use `Noun.verb(...).verb(...)` chains. Example:
  PRReview
    .parallelize(
        run_lint(pr_id),
        run_tests(pr_id),
        check_security(pr_id))
    .if(any check has issues): post_to_slack(channel, message)
    .else: approve_pr(pr_id)

Rules:
- Use `.parallelize(a, b, c)` for tools that can run concurrently (independent inputs).
- Use `.sequence(a, b)` or chained verbs for ordered steps.
- Use `.if(condition): action` for conditional dispatch.
- Inline `// comments` for non-obvious choices.
- Output ONLY the builder spec inside a fenced ```builder block. No prose before or after.
user_prompt:
User goal:
New support ticket came in (id: tk_8842, from: alice@bigco.com, body: "Your
product is broken and I want a refund NOW."). Figure out who they are, what
they want, and how mad they are. If they're a VIP and angry, page our CSM.
If it's a regular billing question, route to finance. If it's spam, drop it.


Available tools:
- classify_intent(text: string) — Classify ticket intent: billing | support | feature_request | spam.
- check_sentiment(text: string) — Sentiment: positive | neutral | negative | angry.
- lookup_customer(email: string) — Get customer record by email. Returns tier (free|pro|vip) and history count.
- page_csm(customer_id: string, reason: string) — Page the on-call customer success manager.
- route_to_finance(ticket_id: string) — Route a billing ticket to the finance team.
- archive_ticket(ticket_id: string) — Archive (drop) a ticket as spam.

Produce the plan now.
plan:
```builder
SupportTicket(id: "tk_8842", from: "alice@bigco.com", body: "Your product is broken and I want a refund NOW.")

  // Gather all intel concurrently — none of these depend on each other
  .parallelize(
    classify_intent("Your product is broken and I want a refund NOW."),
    check_sentiment("Your product is broken and I want a refund NOW."),
    lookup_customer("alice@bigco.com")
  )

  // Decision tree once all three results are in
  .if(classify_intent == "spam"):
    archive_ticket("tk_8842")                          // drop it, no further action

  .else if(classify_intent == "billing"
           AND lookup_customer.tier == "vip"
           AND check_sentiment == "angry"):
    page_csm(
      customer_id: lookup_customer.customer_id,
      reason: "VIP customer alice@bigco.com submitted angry billing/refund ticket tk_8842 — immediate attention required."
    )

  .else if(classify_intent == "billing"):              // billing but not VIP+angry combo
    route_to_finance("tk_8842")

  .else if(lookup_customer.tier == "vip"
           AND check_sentiment == "angry"):            // non-billing but VIP + angry (e.g. support)
    page_csm(
      customer_id: lookup_customer.customer_id,
      reason: "VIP customer alice@bigco.com is angry on ticket tk_8842 (intent: support) — escalate immediately."
    )

  // All other cases (neutral support, feature requests, etc.) — no action defined; fall through
```
